name: Islamic Society of Central Jersey
mission: We aspire to be the anchor of a model community of practicing Muslims of diverse backgrounds, democratically governed, efficiently served, relating to one another with inclusiveness and tolerance, and interacting with the neighbors and the community at large in an Islamic exemplary fashion.‍The fundamental objective of the Society will be to seek Glory to Allah Subhanahu Wa Ta'ala. ‍The Quran, Sunnah and the Sharia shall always be the guiding light for the Society.
theoryOfChange: The Islamic Society of Central Jersey (ISCJ) undertook a year-long strategic planning journey with Oaktree Institute from October 2024 through October 2025. This process aimed to clarify ISCJ's vision, align its leadership, and create a sustainable roadmap for the future. The journey involved working sessions to finalize outcomes, Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), and a multi-year roadmap, focusing on translating vision into tangible action with clear benchmarks for tracking progress.
scoreSummary: Islamic Society of Central Jersey shows good alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: The zakat application is used to apply for Zakat at Islamic Society of Central Jersey (ISCJ). The application must be completed thoroughly and submitted with the appropriate documentation. ‍Online Zakat ApplicationAccess this link to complete your Zakat Application online. (Source: https://www.iscj.org/services/zakat)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 101 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

components.evidence: Accepts zakat (+2); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1); Asnaf: fuqara (+5)
components.improvement_suggestion: Clarify zakat allocation policy and eligible use categories for donors.

components.evidence: Niche cause: RELIGIOUS_CULTURAL (+4); Serves underserved populations (+3)

alignment.rationale: Alignment 32/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, religious cultural cause area

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: Separate restricted/endowment funds from operating reserves and publish a clear reserve-use policy.

judge_issues.message: The rationale mentions "moderate data transparency" but the data confidence badge is MEDIUM and the transparency value is NONE. This is contradictory and should be clarified.

The rationale mentions the program expense ratio being 32.2% and suggests funds are held in reserve or allocated to administrative costs. While true, it doesn't explicitly state that this is a negative factor impacting the score, which it is.

risks.risk_summary: Key concern: Program expense ratio critically low: 32%

The organization is Zakat-eligible and follows specific policies for Zakat and Sadaqah distribution.

The organization provides various community services and programs for its beneficiaries.
ISCJ operates a food pantry and manages the distribution of Zakat and Sadaqah.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate overall score due to its exceptional financial stability and fundraising efficiency, balanced against a lower program expense ratio and moderate data transparency.
areas_for_improvement: program expense ratio of 32.2% is lower than industry benchmarks for direct service delivery
Limited third-party impact data regarding long-term beneficiary outcomes

dimension_explanations.alignment: ISCJ demonstrates a medium donor fit by addressing urgent religious and cultural needs through Zakat-eligible programs [2][8]. The organization has a long history of community involvement, including a 39-year track record of integrating women into leadership roles as trustees and overseers [11][13].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization focuses on capacity building through its endowment fund and direct relief services like its food pantry [4][6]. While it maintains a strong financial position with total revenue of $2,240,124 and 101.2 months of working capital, the program expense ratio is 32.2%, suggesting a significant portion of funds are held in reserve or allocated to administrative costs [1][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Islamic Society of Central Jersey provides essential religious, social, and cultural services to the Muslim community through robust charitable programs.
strengths: Maintains an exceptional working capital reserve of 101.2 months
Highly efficient fundraising operations with a cost of $0.00 per $1 raised
Verified Zakat-eligible status with clear distribution policies
baseline_narrative.summary: The Islamic Society of Central Jersey (ISCJ) is a non-profit organization dedicated to providing social and religious services to the Muslim community [5]. The organization manages critical community initiatives including a food pantry and the distribution of Zakat and Sadaqah to those in need [2][8].

areas_for_improvement.context: The <cite id="3">program expense ratio of 32.2%</cite> is significantly below the <cite id="1">peer median of 85.3%</cite>, suggesting a high portion of funds are held in reserve or used for administration.
The organization could improve by <cite id="1">publishing consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for cost-effectiveness assessments.

case_against.summary: The primary concern is the <cite id="3">low program expense ratio (32.2%)</cite>, which indicates that a minority of annual revenue is spent on direct programs.

confidence.data_freshness: Website data is current (2025), but financial filings are dated (2017)

confidence.notes: Strong historical data and program descriptions, but financial efficiency metrics rely on older filings.

alignment.explanation: Strong alignment for Muslim donors due to <cite id="2">verified zakat eligibility</cite> and a focus on <cite id="2">underserved religious and cultural needs</cite>.
alignment.improvement: Clarifying the <cite id="2">specific zakat allocation policy</cite> would further improve donor confidence in religious compliance.

credibility.explanation: ISCJ is a highly credible institution with a <cite id="5">53-year track record</cite> and <cite id="9">documented governance structures</cite> including diverse trustee representation.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the organization should <cite id="1">separate restricted endowment funds from operating reserves</cite> in public reporting.

impact.explanation: Impact is driven by a <cite id="1">capacity-building model</cite> and <cite id="4">direct services like food pantries</cite>, though efficiency is hampered by the <cite id="3">32.2% program ratio</cite>.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="1">publishing regular beneficiary counts</cite> to justify the current spending model.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value long-term institutional stability and want to support a well-established community hub with a multi-decade track record.
donor_motivations: Want to support a permanent Islamic institution in the US
Value organizations with zero fundraising overhead
Prioritize zakat-eligible community services like food pantries
Prefer established organizations over newer startups
giving_considerations: A large portion of funds are held in reserve rather than spent immediately
Revenue has shown a <cite id="3">3-year CAGR of -7.6%</cite>
Focus is on local community capacity rather than international relief
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking high-velocity disbursement of funds or those who prioritize high program-to-admin ratios above all else.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified program existence and internal tracking, but lacks <cite id="1">publicly available cost-per-beneficiary data</cite>.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: ISCJ creates impact by providing essential religious infrastructure and social safety nets (food, zakat) to maintain community cohesion and spiritual well-being.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that community services are reaching the intended asnaf categories effectively.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: Despite large reserves, the <cite id="3">declining revenue trend (-7.6% CAGR)</cite> suggests a need for renewed donor support to maintain services.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains <cite id="1">101.2 months of operating reserves</cite>, providing a massive buffer against economic volatility.
ISCJ achieves a <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, meaning every dollar donated is acquired without direct marketing expense.
The charity has <cite id="2">verified zakat-eligible status</cite> and serves <cite id="2">eight specific asnaf categories</cite> including the poor and needy.

strengths_deep_dive: ISCJ's long-term institutional strength is rooted in its <cite id="5">founding in 1973</cite> and its accumulation of <cite id="3">over $16 million in total assets</cite>. This financial foundation supports a wide range of community-specific services, from <cite id="7">funeral services</cite> to <cite id="6">business directories</cite>. The organization's <cite id="10">21 active committees</cite> reflect a high level of community engagement and volunteer-led capacity building.
rich_narrative.summary: Founded in <cite id="5">1973</cite>, the Islamic Society of Central Jersey (ISCJ) serves as a cornerstone for the Muslim community, providing <cite id="5">social, religious, and cultural services</cite>. The organization operates with a significant financial base, reporting <cite id="3">total revenue of $2,240,124</cite> and managing <cite id="3">total assets of $16,726,193</cite>. Its mission is executed through a diverse array of programs, including <cite id="4">zakat and sadaqah distribution</cite>, a <cite id="8">food pantry</cite>, and an <cite id="4">endowment fund (Waqf)</cite>. Beyond basic relief, ISCJ fosters community development through initiatives like <cite id="6">Learning Islam Together (LIT)</cite> and a <cite id="6">weekend school</cite>. The organization maintains an <cite id="1">exceptional working capital reserve of 101.2 months</cite>, ensuring long-term institutional stability. While its <cite id="3">program expense ratio is 32.2%</cite>, which is lower than the <cite id="1">industry standard of 75%</cite>, ISCJ demonstrates high efficiency in resource acquisition, maintaining a <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>. The society also emphasizes inclusive governance, with a documented history of <cite id="9">23 women serving as Trustees or Overseers</cite> over its nearly 50-year history.
